_comment: Advisory only. day injects context; it never gates, denies, or rejects an agent action. No hook here may ever emit a blocking decision -- tests/plugin.rs enforces that, and docs/TELOS.md explains why (affordance, not enforcement: crosslink's blocking hooks are the friction this tool exists to avoid repeating).
_why_no_session_end: day hook session-end exists as a command but is deliberately NOT registered. Only UserPromptSubmit, UserPromptExpansion and SessionStart add hook stdout to the MODEL's context; SessionEnd stdout goes to the debug log, so a prompt for the agent registered there would reach nobody. The one mechanism that would deliver text to the model at that moment is Stop's blocking decision, which affordance-not-enforcement forbids. The useful content moved into the SessionStart hook above. CORRECTED (v0.6 harness verification): this reasoning was right about the MODEL and wrong about the HUMAN -- SessionEnd stderr IS shown to the user, so an end-of-session report aimed at a person is achievable and was ruled out by a question nobody asked. Being reconsidered in v0.6 alongside the status line.
